summary_one_line: A devicelink profile whose readable tag's on-disk true type is NOT cmsSigLut16Type ('mft2'), so OriginalType != Lut16 holds.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A profile that parses far enough to read a tag back (device class 'link'/'abst', valid acsp magic, a non-Lut16 LUT tag) so that _cmsGetTagTrueType returns a tag type signature OTHER than cmsSigLut16Type. This requires several 4-byte signature fields (device class at 0x0c, colorspace fields at 0x10-0x17, and the tag-true-type discriminator) to hold specific structural values rather than the still-blocked 'mntr'/Lut16 layout.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 trials and reaches the true side (avg hitcount 14, blocked only 6.5h). Its winning seeds carry device-class bytes 'link' (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and varied colorspace signatures, and offset 0x2d flips to 01 vs the loser's 00 — distinct multi-byte signature substitutions consistent with I2SRandReplace splicing a logged tag-type/signature operand into the input at the offsets the parser compares.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves 0/10, stays blocked all 24h with hitcount 0 on the branch. Its blocking seeds keep device class 'mntr' (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and offset 0x2d=00, so the tag read either fails or yields a Lut16 (mft2) true type, leaving OriginalType == cmsSigLut16Type and the true branch unreached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile. I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the input bytes matching one operand of an intercepted signature/type comparison, and substitutes the other operand, planting the exact 4-byte signature(s) the parser needs to make _cmsGetTagTrueType return a non-Lut16 type. value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient cannot place an exact multi-byte signature, so it never crosses.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same non-Lut16-true-type profile structure, but reached via incremental Hamming/prefix-distance progress on the chain of signature/equality comparisons (device class, colorspace, tag type) so that intermediate near-matches are retained as corpus and refined toward the exact non-Lut16 layout.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 vs cmplog's 2/10 (prob_div 0.70, hit_div 8); its CMP_MAP feedback preserves seeds whose 4-byte signature fields (0x0c-0x12) are progressively closer to a valid non-Lut16 tag layout, building the corpus that the I2S stage then completes.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked ~20h with branch hitcount 6 — it has I2S but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient, so near-miss signature seeds that don't change an edge bucket are discarded and the search loses the partial-progress scaffold value_profile_cmplog keeps.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient over cmplog. Each signature/type CMP writes a (PC, distance-bucket) entry; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus. This retains the intermediate near-match profiles that cmplog throws away, giving the combined fuzzer a refinable foothold on the multi-field signature gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker's true side is taken only when the read tag's true type is not Lut16, and that side is globally blocked.
OriginalType is the on-disk true type of the read tag, so the feature is a profile whose tag true type differs from Lut16.
value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile decisively at this branch (the I2S delta pair).
The winner resolves 9/10 with hitcount 14 while the value_profile loser stays blocked 10/10 with hitcount 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=9/10  blocked=1  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry device class 'link' at the start of the profile while loser seeds carry 'mntr'.
Offset 0x2d is concentrated at 01 in winners but 00 in all losers, a clean dataflow signal.
Winning seeds contain the ASCII 'link' device-class signature.
Loser-blocking seeds contain the 'mntr' device-class signature instead.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand at the input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot place an exact multi-byte signature.
value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog decisively at this branch (the value_profile delta pair).
cmplog resolves only 2/10 vs the winner's 9/10 at this branch.
value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves any input whose operand pair is closer to matching than previously seen, giving incremental progress.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2184 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed d5bed00975df915d` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 15 of the 16-level ancestor chain; tool printed 'I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 15', confirming I2S contribution under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on `OriginalType != cmsSigLut16Type` where value_profile_cmplog and value_profile resolve at equal rates (no I2S advantage) AND cmplog matches value_profile_cmplog (no CMP_MAP advantage), showing the multi-byte signature gate is crossable by havoc alone.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact program-side role of offset 0x2d (01 vs 00) is inferred from the byte-diff concentration, not from a confirmed source field mapping to the tag-type discriminator.
